questions.question: What is the main purpose of the provider adapter pattern?
options: To unify different external APIs behind a common interface
To replace all external APIs with local implementations
To eliminate the need for API keys
questions.explanation: Provider adapters abstract different external services behind a unified interface, allowing the platform to switch providers without changing consumer code.

questions.question: Which resilience pattern prevents cascading failures when an API is down?
questions.explanation: The circuit breaker pattern stops requests to a failing service, preventing cascading failures and giving the service time to recover.

questions.question: Why add jitter to exponential backoff?
options: To make the code more complex
To prevent thundering herd when a service recovers
questions.explanation: Jitter randomizes retry intervals, preventing all clients from retrying simultaneously when a service comes back online (thundering herd problem).
